_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about different types of landscapes, the landform features that make them distinctive, and where they are found in Australia and around the world.

_source.w: We are learning about different types of landscapes, the landform features that make them distinctive, and where they are found in Australia and around the world.
y: Landscapes are the foundation of the physical world we live in — understanding them helps us make sense of our environment.
Knowing about landform types and distribution builds our geographical literacy and sense of place.
This knowledge helps us understand why human settlements and activities are located where they are.
eg: Identifying and describing different landscape types — mountains, plains, plateaus, river valleys, and coastal landscapes.
Mapping the distribution of major landscape types across Australia.
Comparing Australian landscapes with those found on other continents.
Investigating the distinctive features of a specific landscape type — like the Great Dividing Range or the outback plains.
Discussing how landscapes influence where people choose to live and what activities they pursue.
Using photographs and satellite imagery to identify and classify landscape types.
